title: Collapse Propagation Control
purpose: Visualize how mobility business collapse propagates across ODD, Fleet, Queue, Node, Energy, Vehicle and PL layers.

layers.trigger: Weather / road condition reduces autonomous driving availability
ODD restriction reduces usable vehicle pool
layers.impact: Dispatch capacity and ETA reliability decline
Node queue and passenger waiting time increase

layers.trigger: ETA delay and price increase reduce conversion
layers.impact: Demand conversion and load factor decline

layers.trigger: Lower utilization and higher operating cost
Business feasibility falls below control threshold
layers.impact: New dispatch authorization is held until constraints recover

vehicleIntegration.risk: Higher weight reduces payload and increases energy consumption
vehicleIntegration.propagation: Vehicle weight → payload → fleet capacity → H2 demand → node queue → PL

vehicleIntegration.risk: Higher floor height reduces usability and service fit
vehicleIntegration.propagation: Floor height → UX/load efficiency → demand conversion → PL

vehicleIntegration.risk: Fuel cell / battery / hydrogen architecture affects refill time and uptime
vehicleIntegration.propagation: Energy system → refill time → node capacity → fleet availability → dispatch feasibility

decisionRules.condition: All layers remain within operational and business thresholds
One or more layers are degraded but recoverable through reallocation
ODD, energy, fleet, or business feasibility falls below minimum operating condition
